examples.title: Pseudocode: K-Means Algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM K-Means
INPUT: dataset, k (number of clusters)
OUTPUT: cluster assignments, centroids

BEGIN
 // Initialization
 centroids = initializeCentroids(dataset, k)

 // Repeat until convergence
 WHILE NOT converged DO
 // Assignment step
 FOR EACH data point IN dataset DO
 closestCentroid = findClosestCentroid(data point, centroids)
 assign data point to cluster of closestCentroid
 END FOR

 // Update step
 FOR EACH centroid IN centroids DO
 update centroid as mean of assigned data points
 END FOR
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode outlines the K-Means algorithm's main steps: initialization, assignment, and update. It iteratively refines cluster assignments until convergence.

examples.title: Python: K-Means Algorithm
examples.explanation: This Python implementation of the K-Means algorithm includes functions for initialization, finding the closest centroid, updating centroids, and the main K-Means function.
